entries.title: Wayback CDX URL harvest (matchType=domain)
entries.body:
Downloads **every URL of the domain (and its subdomains)** that the Internet Archive has ever seen, via the CDX API. Fully passive: not a single request to the target itself. `matchType=domain` grabs both the domain and all subdomains; `fl=original` returns only the URLs themselves; `collapse=urlkey` collapses duplicates. This is a goldmine: old endpoints, forgotten parameters, deleted pages.

> Correct query form: `url=DOMAIN&matchType=domain` (NOT `url=*.DOMAIN/*`). The CDX status field is called `statuscode`. `fl=original` - output only the URL column.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# cdx_harvest.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -euo pipefail
DOMAIN="${1:?usage: cdx_harvest.sh <domain>}"
OUT="cdx_${DOMAIN}.txt"
UA="ARS3NAL-recon/1.0"

curl -fsS -A "$UA" -G "https://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx" \
  --data-urlencode "url=${DOMAIN}" \
  --data-urlencode "matchType=domain" \
  --data-urlencode "collapse=urlkey" \
  --data-urlencode "fl=original" \
  --data-urlencode "output=text" \
  | sort -u > "$OUT"

echo "[+] $(wc -l < "$OUT") URLs -> $OUT"
```

Run:
```bash
chmod +x cdx_harvest.sh
./cdx_harvest.sh target.com
```

entries.title: Wayback CDX sensitive files (server-side filter)
entries.body:
Searches the archive for **sensitive files** (`.env`, `.git`, `.sql`, `.bak`, `config`, `.json` with keys, etc.), filtering right on the CDX server side - faster and less traffic than pulling everything and grepping locally. Uses the server-side `filter=statuscode:200` (only the ones that actually existed, not 404s) and `filter=original:<regex>` by extension.

> CDX applies a Java regex and **anchors it to the whole field**, so the pattern needs a trailing `.*`: the correct form is `filter=original:.*\.env.*`, otherwise there will be no matches. Each extension is a separate request (CDX handles one filter on the original field at a time more reliably than an alternation).

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# cdx_sensitive.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -uo pipefail
DOMAIN="${1:?usage: cdx_sensitive.sh <domain>}"
OUT="cdx_sensitive_${DOMAIN}.txt"
UA="ARS3NAL-recon/1.0"
: > "$OUT"

# regex anchors to the whole original field -> trailing .* is MANDATORY
PATTERNS=(
  '.*\.env.*' '.*\.git.*' '.*\.sql.*' '.*\.bak.*' '.*\.old.*'
  '.*\.zip.*' '.*\.tar\.gz.*' '.*\.json.*' '.*\.yml.*' '.*\.yaml.*'
  '.*\.config.*' '.*\.ini.*' '.*\.log.*' '.*backup.*' '.*\.swp.*'
)

for pat in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  echo "[*] $pat" >&2
  curl -fsS -A "$UA" -G "https://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx" \
    --data-urlencode "url=${DOMAIN}" \
    --data-urlencode "matchType=domain" \
    --data-urlencode "collapse=urlkey" \
    --data-urlencode "fl=original" \
    --data-urlencode "filter=statuscode:200" \
    --data-urlencode "filter=original:${pat}" \
    --data-urlencode "output=text" 2>/dev/null >> "$OUT" || true
done

sort -u -o "$OUT" "$OUT"
echo "[+] $(wc -l < "$OUT") sensitive URLs -> $OUT" >&2
```

Run:
```bash
./cdx_sensitive.sh target.com
```

entries.title: URL harvest pipeline (gau + waybackurls + katana -> uro -> gf -> qsreplace)
entries.deps: go install github.com/lc/gau/v2/cmd/gau@latest · go install github.com/tomnomnom/waybackurls@latest · go install github.com/projectdiscovery/katana/cmd/katana@latest · pipx install uro · go install github.com/tomnomnom/gf@latest · go install github.com/tomnomnom/qsreplace@latest
entries.body:
A full URL pipeline for bug bounty: collects URLs from all sources (gau - archives/CommonCrawl/OTX, waybackurls - Wayback, katana - active crawling), cleans and deduplicates with **uro** (collapses similar parameterized URLs), classifies by vulnerability class with **gf** (sqli/xss/ssrf/redirect/lfi/ssti/idor) and prepares for fuzzing with **qsreplace FUZZ** (all parameter values get replaced with the `FUZZ` marker).

> gau is installed from the **lc/gau/v2** repo (`go install github.com/lc/gau/v2/cmd/gau@latest`), not tomnomnom. gf needs patterns: `git clone https://github.com/1ndianl33t/Gf-Patterns ~/.gf`. katana makes ACTIVE requests to the target - run it only within scope.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# url_harvest.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -uo pipefail
DOMAIN="${1:?usage: url_harvest.sh <domain>}"
DIR="urls_${DOMAIN}"; mkdir -p "$DIR"
have() { command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; }
TMP="$(mktemp -d)"; trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT

echo "[1/4] collect" >&2
have gau         && gau --subs "$DOMAIN"            >> "$TMP/raw" 2>/dev/null
have waybackurls && echo "$DOMAIN" | waybackurls   >> "$TMP/raw" 2>/dev/null
have katana      && katana -silent -d 2 -u "https://$DOMAIN" >> "$TMP/raw" 2>/dev/null

echo "[2/4] dedup (uro)" >&2
if have uro; then uro < "$TMP/raw" | sort -u > "$DIR/all_urls.txt"
else sort -u "$TMP/raw" > "$DIR/all_urls.txt"; fi
echo "    $(wc -l < "$DIR/all_urls.txt") urls" >&2

echo "[3/4] gf classify" >&2
if have gf; then
  for p in sqli xss ssrf redirect lfi ssti idor rce; do
    gf "$p" < "$DIR/all_urls.txt" 2>/dev/null | sort -u > "$DIR/gf_${p}.txt" || true
  done
fi

echo "[4/4] qsreplace FUZZ" >&2
if have qsreplace; then
  grep '?' "$DIR/all_urls.txt" | qsreplace FUZZ 2>/dev/null | sort -u > "$DIR/fuzz_urls.txt"
fi
echo "[+] done -> $DIR/" >&2
```

Run:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/1ndianl33t/Gf-Patterns ~/.gf   # once
./url_harvest.sh target.com
```

entries.title: Recover deleted pages from Wayback (raw id_ snapshots)
entries.body:
Pulls **currently deleted/unavailable** pages straight from the archive in raw form. First it fetches every snapshot with `statuscode:200` from CDX, takes the freshest timestamp for each URL, then downloads the **raw** content using the special `id_` modifier in the archive URL (`web.archive.org/web/<ts>id_/<url>`) - this returns the original response without the Wayback toolbar wrapper. Handy for recovering old JS, a config, or an endpoint that has already been stripped from the live site.

> The `id_` (identity) modifier is the key bit: without it the archive injects its own JS/banner and corrupts the files. Only snapshots with `statuscode:200` are taken, so archived 404s are not pulled.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# recover_deleted.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -uo pipefail
DOMAIN="${1:?usage: recover_deleted.sh <domain> [maxfetch]}"
MAX="${2:-25}"
DIR="recovered_${DOMAIN}"; mkdir -p "$DIR"
UA="ARS3NAL-recon/1.0"

# timestamp + original, successful snapshots only
curl -fsS -A "$UA" -G "https://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx" \
  --data-urlencode "url=${DOMAIN}" \
  --data-urlencode "matchType=domain" \
  --data-urlencode "collapse=urlkey" \
  --data-urlencode "filter=statuscode:200" \
  --data-urlencode "fl=timestamp,original" \
  --data-urlencode "output=text" 2>/dev/null > "$DIR/_index.txt" || true

echo "[*] $(wc -l < "$DIR/_index.txt") snapshots, fetching up to $MAX raw" >&2
n=0
while read -r ts url; do
  [ -z "${url:-}" ] && continue
  n=$((n+1)); [ "$n" -gt "$MAX" ] && break
  fname="$DIR/$(echo "$url" | sed 's#https\?://##; s#[^A-Za-z0-9._-]#_#g' | cut -c1-120)"
  # id_ = raw original response without the Wayback toolbar
  curl -fsSL -A "$UA" "https://web.archive.org/web/${ts}id_/${url}" -o "$fname" 2>/dev/null || true
  echo "    [$n] $url" >&2
done < "$DIR/_index.txt"
echo "[+] raw files -> $DIR/" >&2
```

Run:
```bash
./recover_deleted.sh target.com 50
```
